papers.title: A 3nm GAAFET Physical Design Flow with Backside Power Delivery (BSPDN) Co-Optimization
papers.summary: This work presents a complete standard cell design and routing flow utilizing nano-TSVs for backside power grid synthesis in sub-3nm gate-all-around nodes. By isolating VDD/VSS grids onto the wafer backside, frontside routing congestion is reduced, and maximum current density electromigration hazards are minimized under high-frequency operation.
papers.applications: Directly applicable to next-generation high-frequency mobile application processors and ultra-dense server processors targeting TSMC N2 and Intel 18A physical design envelopes.
papers.industryRelevance: Demonstrates a 14% chip area reduction and a 6% reduction in dynamic voltage IR-drop across the standard cell grid during maximum clock cycle loads.

papers.title: RV64GCH Vector Extension Integration and Out-of-Order Execution Hazard Mitigation
papers.summary: An evaluation of speculative vector execution pipelines in a dual-issue out-of-order 64-bit core. The paper details custom register renaming mapping and speculative vector store-to-load forwarding queues designed to eliminate execution bubbles during deep matrix multipliers.
papers.applications: Improves execution pipeline density for edge neural network model accelerators and custom DSP systems running on standard open ISA pipelines.
papers.industryRelevance: Boosts cryptographic hashing and sparse floating-point arithmetic performance by 1.82x while keeping physical gate count increases under 8%.
